action.reasoning: Observation: the frozen-CLIP feature pipeline failed immediately because train.py wrapped the model in DataParallel and then called model.encode_image, which is not exposed on DataParallel. Analysis: this is a simple implementation bug, not a modeling failure. Given the previous end-to-end CLIP run was far too slow and poor, the frozen-feature approach is still the right direction because it should cut iteration time dramatically. We should fix DataParallel access, finish the feature-extraction + linear-head training pipeline, add a local val-format smoke test for predict.py, and submit eval right away to get a new baseline and timing. Decision: patch train.py to use a helper that accesses module.encode_image under DataParallel, keep frozen CLIP with balanced class weights, train for a few epochs, run predict.py on val split locally, then submit eval.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 12 --batch_size 256 --lr 3e-3 --num_workers 12 --save_path checkpoints/best.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output val_preds.json
